front: Short Story: A 'Catholic Diabolist' who wrote the poetry collection Fungoids makes a deal with the devil to visit the British Museum Reading Room on June 3, 1997, only to find he is remembered solely as 'an immajnari karrakter in Inglish Littracher'
Short Story: The title poet is described in a future encyclopedia as 'a thurd-rait poit hoo beleevz imself a grate jeneus,' having written Fungoids and Negations
Short Story: In this Beerbohm story, Enoch dismisses Percy Bysshe Shelley as 'second-rate' and accuses him of possessing 'a deadly evenness'
Novel: All the undergraduates at Oxford University commit suicide out of love for the title femme fatale and bumbling magician
Novel: In this Beerbohm novel, busts of twelve Roman emperors sweat 'great beads of perspiration' upon seeing the title character
Novel: Two black owls perching on the battlements of Tankerton Hall foretell the Duke of Dorset's drowning in the River Isis in this novel
Short Story: The narrator tries to complete a ludicrous verse drama in which the Guelfs and Ghibellines fight, Michelangelo and Benvenuto Cellini appear, and Pippa passes
Short Story: The title palmist foresees his own injury and his companions' deaths in a train accident through palmistry but refuses to act to prevent it
Short Story: In this Beerbohm story, the narrator imagines a conversation between a very young envelope and a very old envelope
Short Story: Lord George Hell is rejected by Jenny Mere for not having 'the faint of a saint'; first published in The Yellow Book
Short Story: In this Beerbohm story, the protagonist changes his surname from 'Hell' to 'Heaven'
Collection: This Beerbohm parody anthology parodied 17 contemporaries, including a mock Rudyard Kipling poem about Santa Claus being arrested
Collection: This Beerbohm collection of 23 caricatures lampoons Dante Gabriel Rossetti and the Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood

Author: This British author was the half-brother of actor and theater manager Herbert Beerbohm Tree, who premiered the roles of Henry Higgins in Pygmalion and Lord Illingworth in A Woman of No Importance
Author: This author established his reputation as a caricaturist with The Poets' Corner, depicting 'twenty-five gentlemen' including Aubrey Beardsley

Short Story: A character is repeatedly startled by a doppelganger of his rival, causing him to cut himself shaving and spill borscht on his dress shirt at the Duchess of Hertfordshire's estate at Keeb
